title: marcetia viscida
intro: Marcetia viscida, also known as the viscid Marcetia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Calycopodiaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to South America, particularly in countries such as Brazil and Paraguay. Marcetia viscida has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, and can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for herbal consumption.
